title: Cipher Mining Signs 168 MW, 10-Year AI Hosting Agreement
text: Cipher Mining announced a decade-long partnership with Fluidstack to deliver high-performance computing infrastructure. The arrangement involves deploying 168 megawatts of critical IT capacity at Cipher's Barber Lake facility in Colorado City, Texas, with potential expansion to 244 megawatts. The deal secures approximately $3 billion in revenue over its initial ten-year period, with optional extensions that could push total contract value to around $7 billion. The project is slated for completion by September 2026. Google will backstop $1.4 billion of Fluidstack's lease commitments and receive warrants representing roughly 5.4% equity ownership in Cipher. The arrangement supports project-related debt financing while allowing Cipher to retain full project ownership. The site is projected to generate an 80-85% net operating income margin, with development costs estimated between $9-$11 million per megawatt. CEO Tyler Page stated, This transformative transaction reinforces our HPC momentum as we continue to attract attention for our large and growing pipeline of sites. The agreement positions both companies to address escalating AI infrastructure demands while establishing Cipher as a significant player in the data center development landscape.
